description: Sub-product invariance test at EVERY MLP layer of every model: compare per-neuron DLA-profile correlations for expressions sharing the same b*c sub-product vs different sub-products (Pearson r; Welch t + Mann-Whitney U; headline Cohen's d, positive = rule-like/abstract, negative = surface-form/operand-pair lookup), Holm-Bonferroni corrected. Pre-FT and post-FT (multi-seed). Driver: modal_app.py rule_vs_lookup_exp_g_sweep_suite.
motivation: Replace the fragile one-layer verdict with a full-depth profile, and test cross-model generality.
formal_results: A shared five-band profile: early baseline (d>0) -> mid surface-form band (d<<0; Qwen-7B pre min d=-2.20 at L20, p=5e-32) -> transition (d~0) -> late abstract-b*c band (d>0; L23 d=+0.65) -> assembly tail. Same band IDENTITY across Qwen-7B/1.5B/DeepSeek (DeepSeek late band genuinely staged, L23 d=+0.59); they differ only in band depth/location.
results_description: Lookup-vs-rule is distributed across depth as a five-band parse->surface-form->transition->abstract->assembly profile, shared across math LMs.
conclusions: The single-layer rule-vs-lookup verdict was a collapse of a distributed, layered phenomenon; the layered sub-product profile is the headline structure and it generalizes across families/scales.
